title: teucrium eriocephalum subsp. eriocephalum
intro: Teucrium eriocephalum subsp. eriocephalum, also known as the woolly germander, is a perennial plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off bacterial infections, due to its high content of essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Teucrium eriocephalum subsp. eriocephalum include its potential to improve digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular herb in traditional medicine. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for various forms of consumption and administration.
